intro: Cohniella wittii, also known as Witt's rockrose or Wittia, is a flowering plant species within the family Cistaceae. Traditionally, Cohniella wittii has been used to support digestive health and relieve symptoms of nausea, flatulence, and diarrhea due to its potential anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. The bioactive constituents of Cohniella wittii include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties. Cohniella wittii is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to support digestive health and alleviate gastrointestinal issues.
